<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="g1" x1="190" y1="90" x2="280" y2="420" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#bfe7ff" stop-opacity="0.65"/>
      <stop offset="0.55" stop-color="#7fc9ff" stop-opacity="0.25"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#4aa6ff" stop-opacity="0.12"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="rim" x1="130" y1="135" x2="370" y2="135" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#dff6ff" stop-opacity="0.95"/>
      <stop offset="0.5" stop-color="#bdeeff" stop-opacity="0.6"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#dff6ff" stop-opacity="0.9"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="shine" cx="0.28" cy="0.2" r="0.7">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#ffffff" stop-opacity="0.55"/>
      <stop offset="0.25" stop-color="#ffffff" stop-opacity="0.25"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#ffffff" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-20%" y="-20%" width="140%" height="140%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="2.2"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- ground shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="430" rx="120" ry="24" fill="#1b2a3a" opacity="0.18" filter="url(#soft)"/>

  <!-- glass silhouette -->
  <path d="M165 120
           L197 370
           C199 386 210 395 224 395
           L288 395
           C302 395 313 386 315 370
           L347 120
           Z" fill="url(#g1)" stroke="#7bd0ff" stroke-opacity="0.7" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- rim -->
  <path d="M175 105
           Q256 70 337 105
           L320 130
           Q256 105 192 130
           Z" fill="url(#rim)" stroke="#8de0ff" stroke-width="4" stroke-opacity="0.75"/>

  <!-- inner highlight area -->
  <path d="M205 140
           Q230 190 225 310
           Q222 365 216 378
           C240 386 270 386 292 378
           Q279 350 282 310
           Q289 190 315 140
           Q278 165 260 165
           Q235 165 205 140 Z" fill="url(#shine)" opacity="0.85"/>

  <!-- left vertical reflection -->
  <path d="M205 150
           C200 190 198 250 205 300
           C210 340 211 360 208 386
           C222 392 229 392 240 392
           C234 365 234 332 232 300
           C229 250 232 192 238 150
           Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.18"/>

  <!-- right vertical reflection -->
  <path d="M306 150
           C312 190 314 250 306 300
           C302 340 301 360 305 386
           C291 392 284 392 273 392
           C279 365 279 332 281 300
           C284 250 281 192 276 150
           Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.12"/>

  <!-- water line / meniscus (to suggest glass content) -->
  <path d="M205 320
           C235 300 277 300 307 320
           C305 335 286 344 256 344
           C226 344 207 335 205 320 Z" fill="#66c6ff" opacity="0.18" stroke="#9be2ff" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- small sparkle dots -->
  <g opacity="0.55">
    <circle cx="210" cy="240" r="3.5" fill="#ffffff"/>
    <circle cx="238" cy="205" r="2.6" fill="#ffffff"/>
    <circle cx="296" cy="215" r="2.8" fill="#ffffff"/>
    <circle cx="270" cy="250" r="2.3" fill="#ffffff"/>
  </g>

  <!-- subtle bottom rim -->
  <path d="M205 395
           C220 408 235 414 256 414
           C277 414 292 408 307 395" fill="none" stroke="#9be2ff" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="4"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating a glass representation**

I need to draw the word "glass" visually without using letters. I’m thinking of representing it as a tumbler, capturing its transparency and reflections to convey the concept effectively. My plan is to create an SVG image that's 512 pixels in size. It’ll be interesting to focus on the visual aspects rather than textual ones, so I want to ensure I get those details right in the drawing!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

**Generating one-word guesses**

I'm thinking of starting my list of one-word guesses related to a glass of water. I'll begin with "water," "glass," "cup," "tumbler," "beverage," "drink," "liquid," and "H2O." I want to make sure there are no repeats, so I'll avoid numbers and stay away from guessing things like "goblet." My goal is to come up with a total of 20 unique words. Let's keep going!
